the concentration of the pollutant at time t and distance x is modeled by the partial differential equation:  
∂p/∂t = d ∂²p/∂x² - v ∂p/∂x - k p  
given d = 0.05 km²/day, v = 1 km/day, and k = 0.1 day⁻¹, the solution to the equation is:  
p(t, x) = e^{-x²} e^{-t}  
to mitigate the pollution, the facilities are installed at positions x₁, x₂, x₃ such that the total concentration at the intake point x = 10 km is minimized.  
the optimal positions are x₁ = 2, x₂ = 5, x₃ = 8  
the total concentration at the intake point is:  
p(10, 10) = e^{-10²} e^{-10}
